representative_artifacts.observation: The five labeled instants clearly show the orange mu=0.4 cube moving downhill and out of the crop while the green mu=0.5 cube remains in its initial region through t=2.00 s.
The decoded 61-frame, 30 fps clip visibly preserves the progressive orange-slide versus green-stick separation; the media alone is not used to prove the physical trace contract.
At step 0 the orange low-friction and green high-friction cubes occupy their aligned starting regions on the two presentation-offset incline cells.
At step 30 the orange cube has begun a visible downhill separation while the green cube remains visually fixed relative to its board.
At step 60 the orange cube is substantially farther downhill, with the stationary green cube providing an unambiguous visual reference.
At step 90 the orange cube has nearly left the cropped world view while the green cube still occupies its starting region.
At step 120 the orange slide cube has exited the crop and the green threshold-stick cube remains visible in place, completing the visual classification.
